seo_h1: Egyptian Affairs as Recorded by Lyceas of Naucratis

seo.browser_title: Lyceas of Naucratis on Eulogikon Greek Archive Collection
seo.meta_title: Lyceas of Naucratis Ancient Greek Historical Works
seo.meta_description: Lyceas of Naucratis, a 4th century BCE historian. His surviving historical fragments are preserved. Free to read and download from the Greek library archive.

biography_html:
Lyceas of Naucratis was a Greek historian from the Egyptian city of Naucratis, active in the 4th century BCE. No biographical details survive. His existence is attested solely by a single citation in the geographer Strabo, which identifies him as a source for Egyptian history.

His only known work is a lost History of Egypt, preserved in a single fragment concerning the geography of the Nile Delta. Lyceas’s significance lies entirely in his citation by Strabo. He exemplifies the category of lost local historians from the Hellenistic period whose works were utilized by later compilers.

His mention confirms Greek historiographical activity in Naucratis and represents one thread in the transmission of geographical knowledge about Egypt.
